g.data-narration: Welcome to Multimedia in Android. Today we'll explore system architecture, audio/video playback, and Text-to-Speech.
Multimedia combines audio, video, and images. Android provides powerful APIs for media handling in apps.
g.text: Multimedia refers to the combination of audio, video, and image data to create
rich media experiences. Android's multimedia framework enables developers to
play, record, and process various media formats seamlessly.

g.data-narration: Audio/video powers entertainment apps, e-learning, and communication tools. TTS enables accessibility features.
Intent is a message to request actions from app components. Intent Filters specify which components can handle specific intents.
g.text: A message requesting an action from app components
Specifies which components can handle particular intents

g.data-narration: Activity lifecycle has six states: onCreate, onStart, onResume, onPause, onStop, onDestroy. These manage app visibility and resources.
Broadcast receivers have three lifecycle methods: onReceive handles incoming broadcasts, onCreate initializes, onDestroy cleans up.
MediaPlayer is the core class for playing audio and video. It provides methods like setDataSource, prepare, and start.
g.text: Core class for playing audio/video with key methods:

g.data-narration: To play audio, create MediaPlayer instance, set data source, prepare, then start. Supports formats like MP3, WAV, AAC.
AudioFormat defines the audio data format. AudioTrack plays audio data. AudioInputStream reads audio from files.
g.text: Defines audio data format (MP3, WAV, AAC)
Reads audio data from file or stream

g.data-narration: For video, use SurfaceView or VideoView to display content. VideoView provides a simpler high-level interface for video playback.
g.text: Displays video surface for custom rendering
High-level view for displaying video files with built-in controls

g.data-narration: MediaRecorder records audio and video. Configure source, output format, encoder, then call prepare and start to begin recording.
TTS converts text to spoken words. Android provides TextToSpeech and SpeechSynthesizer classes for implementing voice output.
g.text: Converts text into spoken words for accessibility and voice interaction.

g.data-narration: Initialize TextToSpeech with OnInitListener, then call speak method with text, queue mode, and optional parameters.
Key points: Intent and Activity lifecycle manage app components. MediaPlayer plays audio/video. TextToSpeech enables voice output for accessibility.
